title: Response Status, Success Variants, Failure Taxonomy, and Problem Details · Automation and Data Client Systems · Spiral Review
summary: A compliance analyst reconstructing a partial ingestion handoff must reconstruct the governing policy from partial evidence after delay and distinguish current rules from tempting earlier ones for response status, success variants, failure taxonomy, and problem details, producing typed response and failure classifier. Planned artifact: Extend the evidence record for a Python 3.14 urllib and HTTP client layers typed response and failure classifier after a delayed changed case. Learners progress…
objectives: Interpret informational, successful, redirection, client-error, and server-error status codes as protocol metadata. Demonstrate the result through Python 3.14 urllib, http.client, ssl, typed protocols, and fixture transports.

Handle 201, 202, 204, 206, and other success responses with their location, body, and completion implications. Demonstrate the result through Python 3.14 urllib, http.client, ssl, typed protocols, and fixture transports.

Separate request construction, transport, status, representation, and domain failures in a stable client contract. Demonstrate the result through Python 3.14 urllib, http.client, ssl, typed protocols, and fixture transports.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""http-clients-python cumulative safe data-client portfolio.

Browser labs use deterministic request and response models only. Live urllib,
DNS, TLS, sockets, concurrency, and load require local Python 3.14 transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass

@dataclass(frozen=True)
class RequestEvidence:
    method: str
    target: str
    attempt: int

@dataclass(frozen=True)
class ResponseEvidence:
    status: int
    body_bytes: int
    valid: bool

print("Python HTTP client portfolio ready")

steps.title: Python Separate evidence from confidence · Review Partial And Ambiguous Outcomes
